source_jobs.title: Lead Data Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Yobi is a rapidly growing Behavioral AI company on a mission to ethically democratize the benefits of data and AI.



Since 2019, we have built one of the largest consented behavioral datasets in the United States, extending far beyond the walled gardens of Big Tech. Unlike traditional LLM companies, Yobi builds foundation models of human behavior grounded in real-world actions such as purchases and store visits.



Our private-by-design modeling enables state-of-the-art personalization and decisioning for leading brands and agencies while protecting privacy, safety, and ethics.



Today, we are focused on bringing the performance of closed-web user acquisition to the open web and connected TV, giving brands walled-garden results without the walls.



At our core, Yobi is building the behavioral intelligence layer for any system that makes a personalization decision.



Working at Yobi

We’re at an inflection point—customer adoption is accelerating, but there’s still room to shape the architecture and culture from the ground up. Engineers here own major surface areas, build 0→1 systems in large-scale data and model infrastructure, and help define how Behavioral AI scales ethically and effectively.



Highlights:

 - Well-funded with 5+ years of runway. At the same time, we are scaling revenue quickly and project to be breakeven in 2026.

 - Partnerships with Microsoft and Databricks

 - Fully remote or hybrid from several hubs (SF Bay Area, Seattle, NYC)

 - World-class team of Machine Learning experts who worked on cutting edge infra and recommender systems @ Amazon, Uber, Twitter, Meta, etc.

 - Product and Go-To-Market teams who have taken ideas from concept to 9 figure revenue streams
   
   

Benefits:

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Meaningful equity & financial upside - a real % of the company

 - Annual bonus target based on personal and company performance

 - Health, Dental, Vision available

 - Unlimited PTO - we care about impact, not tracking days you’re out

 - 401k with company match %


ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Lead Data Engineer at Yobi, you will be at the center of how data moves, scales, and powers our Behavioral AI products. You will be working on various virtual teams and projects, with an eye towards schema modeling, orchestration, abstractions, workload efficiency, reliability/observability, and so on. This is a hands-on engineering role.




WHAT IT TAKES TO SUCCEED IN THIS ROLE:

 - You think in systems. You’re comfortable designing data workflows that balance clarity, modularity, and performance.
   

 - You’re flexible and opinionated. You’re familiar with many different patterns for instrumenting data pipelines - push based, pull based, eventually consistent, 1 job per partition, batch, streaming, etc. and know when to reach for different patterns.
   

 - You’re ready to influence and lead, aligning other flexible and opinionated engineers from other domains on the right conventions, abstractions, and strategy in an ambiguous and evolving environment.
   

 - You have the hard skills. While we are big believers in “hire the person, not the specific language background”, our current data stack is Delta Lake, Scala Spark, Python Spark, and Airflow - it would be great if you have direct experience with at least some of these.
   

 - You sweat the details. Schema evolution, data contracts, dependency graphs, metadata tracking — you know these are what make data reliable at scale.
   

 - You’re pragmatic - you know when to polish and when to just ship.
   

 - You care about performance. Wall clock time matters for us. Cost also matters for us. We think in many projects, there are opportunities to win on both, with the right data engineering.

We prioritize attitude, culture, and general (technical) fit over matching perfectly into one of our job descriptions. If our mission and work resonates with you, we encourage you to apply. Tell us how you can help drive our products forward, even if you don’t fee

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer - Inference / Serving
source_jobs.description:
Yobi is a rapidly growing Behavioral AI company on a mission to ethically democratize the benefits of data and AI.



Since 2019, we have built one of the largest consented behavioral datasets in the United States, extending far beyond the walled gardens of Big Tech. Unlike traditional LLM companies, Yobi builds foundation models of human behavior grounded in real-world actions such as purchases and store visits.



Our private-by-design modeling enables state-of-the-art personalization and decisioning for leading brands and agencies while protecting privacy, safety, and ethics.



Today, we are focused on bringing the performance of closed-web user acquisition to the open web and connected TV, giving brands walled-garden results without the walls.



At our core, Yobi is building the behavioral intelligence layer for any system that makes a personalization decision.



Working at Yobi

We’re at an inflection point—customer adoption is accelerating, but there’s still room to shape the architecture and culture from the ground up. Engineers here own major surface areas, build 0→1 systems in large-scale data and model infrastructure, and help define how Behavioral AI scales ethically and effectively.



Highlights:

 - Well-funded with 5+ years of runway. At the same time, we are scaling revenue quickly and project to be breakeven in 2026.

 - Partnerships with Microsoft and Databricks

 - Fully remote or hybrid from several hubs (SF Bay Area, Seattle, NYC)

 - World-class team of Machine Learning experts who worked on cutting edge infra and recommender systems @ Amazon, Uber, Twitter, Meta, etc.

 - Product and Go-To-Market teams who have taken ideas from concept to 9 figure revenue streams
   
   

Benefits:

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Meaningful equity & financial upside - a real % of the company

 - Annual bonus target based on personal and company performance

 - Health, Dental, Vision available

 - Unlimited PTO - we care about impact, not tracking days you’re out

 - 401k with company match %


ABOUT THE ROLE

As a Machine Learning Engineer focused on Inference and Serving at Yobi, you’ll design, optimize, and operate the systems that bring our Behavioral AI models to life in real time. You’ll work at the core of our production environment, turning trained models into performant, reliable, and continuously improving services that power our open-web and CTV products.

This is an applied ML systems role—equal parts engineering depth, deployment craft, and model intuition. You’ll shape how models are packaged, versioned, rolled out, and observed across environments, ensuring every prediction is fast, accurate, and accountable.




WHAT IT TAKES TO SUCCEED IN THIS ROLE:

 - Deep expertise in model deployment. You’ve built or scaled production ML serving systems—handling versioning, rollouts, rollback strategies, and live experimentation.
   

 - Low-latency mindset. You understand what makes inference fast: model graph optimization, quantization, caching, batching, and efficient feature retrieval.
   

 - Systems fluency. You write robust, high-performance code in Go, Rust, C++, or Java, and are comfortable bridging to Python for model integration and analysis.
   

 - Operational maturity. You treat inference as a living system—monitoring drift, tracking model lineage, and ensuring observability from input to outcome.
   

 - Infrastructure intuition. You know how to make serving systems reproducible and portable without over-engineering them, whether that’s through custom runtime design, model registries, or lightweight orchestration.
   

 - Applied ML understanding. You can reason about model performance, interpret trade-offs, and work with researchers to make models more deployable.

A reasonable estimate of the current base salary range at the time of posting is below. Base salary does not include other forms of compensation or benefits. Actual base salary within the specified range is comprised of several components

Roles & responsibilities:

 - Improve business intelligence dashboards and provide insights to sales and eng teams

 - Implement a robust sales process by identifying gaps in sales cycles, necessary collateral, and key customer events

 - Support strategic financing initiatives with financial analysis, projections, and due diligence materials

 - Strong analytical expertise, and extensive experience building integrated models and processes from scratch

 - Experience building relationships and communicating with stakeholders across all levels and functions

 - Proven proclivity for quickly identifying and methodically resolving potential road-blocks

Base salary range: $150,000-$200,000

source_jobs.title: Machine Learning Engineer - Core Signals
source_jobs.description:
Yobi is a rapidly growing Behavioral AI company on a mission to ethically democratize the benefits of data and AI.



Since 2019, we have built one of the largest consented behavioral datasets in the United States, extending far beyond the walled gardens of Big Tech. Unlike traditional LLM companies, Yobi builds foundation models of human behavior grounded in real-world actions such as purchases and store visits.



Our private-by-design modeling enables state-of-the-art personalization and decisioning for leading brands and agencies while protecting privacy, safety, and ethics.



Today, we are focused on bringing the performance of closed-web user acquisition to the open web and connected TV, giving brands walled-garden results without the walls.



At our core, Yobi is building the behavioral intelligence layer for any system that makes a personalization decision.



Working at Yobi

We’re at an inflection point—customer adoption is accelerating, but there’s still room to shape the architecture and culture from the ground up. Engineers here own major surface areas, build 0→1 systems in large-scale data and model infrastructure, and help define how Behavioral AI scales ethically and effectively.



Highlights:

 - Well-funded with 5+ years of runway. At the same time, we are scaling revenue quickly and project to be breakeven in 2026.

 - Partnerships with Microsoft and Databricks

 - Fully remote or hybrid from several hubs (SF Bay Area, Seattle, NYC)

 - World-class team of Machine Learning experts who worked on cutting edge infra and recommender systems @ Amazon, Uber, Twitter, Meta, etc.

 - Product and Go-To-Market teams who have taken ideas from concept to 9 figure revenue streams
   
   

Benefits:

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Meaningful equity & financial upside - a real % of the company

 - Annual bonus target based on personal and company performance

 - Health, Dental, Vision available

 - Unlimited PTO - we care about impact, not tracking days you’re out

 - 401k with company match %


ABOUT THE ROLE

As an MLE on the Core Signals team, you will primarily be focused on (1) the representation learning surrounding our fundamental user-behavioral modeling problems and (2) using those core models to power new and existing products.

This role involves a lot of collaboration with the Product org and Applications team to realize these R&D gains - but is ultimately a "full stack" ML role. Day to day responsibilities include data processing, model training, deployment, and evaluation.

A good amount of "wearing your Product hat" is expected, as well as the ability to flex into some other functions as needed - we are a quickly growing startup after all.




WHAT IT TAKES TO SUCCEED IN THIS ROLE:

 - You understand enough about machine learning to be able to apply it to novel problems and suggest improvements to our current setup, or even radical new approaches. Previous publication experience is not required.
   
   

 - You've worked on and can speak to at least some of: representation learning, embeddings drift, CTR modeling, sequence modeling, etc., preferably on "big data" in an industrial setting.
   
   

 - Skill and attitude wise, you can quickly contribute to the non-research components of our pipeline. This includes things such as data orchestration, build systems, and experiment tracking. Although we use a combination of open source products like Airflow, Bazel, Github CI/CD, and Spark, prior experience with these specific solutions is not needed. However, a good part of your day to day will involve interacting with these systems, so you should be comfortable with getting your hands dirty.
   
   

 - Good product sense - you have opinions on what we should and shouldn’t be doing both in chasing product-market fit and on the implementation side.

source_jobs.description:
Yobi is a rapidly growing Behavioral AI company on a mission to ethically democratize the benefits of data and AI.



Since 2019, we have built one of the largest consented behavioral datasets in the United States, extending far beyond the walled gardens of Big Tech. Our private-by-design modeling enables state-of-the-art personalization and decisioning for leading brands and agencies while protecting privacy, safety, and ethics.



Today, we are focused on bringing the performance of closed-web user acquisition to the open web and connected TV, giving brands walled-garden results without the walls.



At our core, Yobi is building the behavioral intelligence layer for any system that makes a personalization decision.



Working at Yobi

We’re at an inflection point—customer adoption is accelerating, but there’s still room to shape the architecture and culture from the ground up. Engineers here own major surface areas, build 0→1 systems in large-scale data and model infrastructure, and help define how Behavioral AI scales ethically and effectively.



Highlights:

 - Well-funded with 5+ years of runway. At the same time, we are scaling revenue quickly and project to be breakeven in 2026.

 - Partnerships with Microsoft and Databricks

 - Fully remote or hybrid from several hubs (SF Bay Area, Seattle, NYC)

 - World-class team of Machine Learning experts who worked on cutting edge infra and recommender systems @ Amazon, Uber, Twitter, Meta, etc.

 - Product and Go-To-Market teams who have taken ideas from concept to 9 figure revenue streams
   
   

Benefits:

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Meaningful equity & financial upside - a real % of the company

 - Annual bonus target based on personal and company performance

 - Health, Dental, Vision available

 - Unlimited PTO - we care about impact, not tracking days you’re out

 - 401k with company match %


ABOUT THE ROLE

As the Tech Lead / Senior Software Engineer for the Yobi Ad Platform, you’ll be responsible for leading the design and implementation of the systems that power our real-time ML-based advertising stack. This is a senior role where you will own a major product bet, mentor other engineers, and drive architectural direction—while still spending more than half of your time building.

You’ll work across low-latency decisioning, data pipelines, and service orchestration layers to make our platform reliable, scalable, and developer-friendly. The work spans real-time bidding systems, cloud-native infrastructure, and reproducible deployment tooling.




WHAT IT TAKES TO SUCCEED IN THIS ROLE:

 - You have experience leading multi-person or even multi-team projects.
   
   

 - You know when to empower/influence another person or team, and when to roll up your sleeves and implement it yourself
   
   

 - Deep systems experience. You’ve built or scaled distributed, high-throughput, low-latency systems—ideally in advertising, recommendations, or another real-time domain.
   
   

 - Strong with compiled languages. You’re fluent in one or more of Go, Rust, C++, Scala, or Java, and write code with clarity, correctness, and performance in mind.
   
   

 - Architectural ownership. You can design clean interfaces, modular service boundaries, and deployment patterns that scale with product and team growth.
   
   

 - Operational maturity. You understand observability, alerting, and reproducibility, and you treat CI/CD and infrastructure as first-class citizens.
   
   

 - Mentorship and leadership. You lead by example, elevate those around you, and turn ambiguity into clarity and action.
   
   

 - Startup mindset. You balance strong technical standards with pragmatism—you know when to ship and when to refactor.

ABOUT THE ROLE

At Yobi, Applications teams bring the value in our User-Behavioral foundation models to market, creating scalable, highly profitable products with ML at their heart. These Applications products are key to Yobi success, as they ground the value of our R&D, power continuous experimentation and improvement, and provide significant data for improving our core embeddings.

As an MLE on this team, you will primarily be focused on the models, metrics, pipelines, systems, and services that power and deliver excellence via Yobi Applications products.

This role involves a large degree of 0-to-1 development, and will rely on collaboration with Product, core signals MLEs, and leaning on your own expertise and insight in building holistic ML-powered products. While we currently have a product in the market here, we invite big bets to expand impact and reach.

Significant "wearing your Product hat" is expected, along with driving results in the many domains required to deliver whole ML-powered products - we are a quickly growing startup after all!




WHAT IT TAKES TO SUCCEED IN THIS ROLE:

 - Understanding enough about machine learning to be dangerous but not necessarily published in the field. This means you have worked on and can speak to impactful consumer-facing ML problems, e.g. recommender systems, personalization, etc. that you have directly contributed to.
   
   

 - Skill and attitude wise, you can quickly contribute to the “full stack” of our pipeline. This includes things such as data orchestration, build systems, and experiment tracking. Although we use a combination of open source products like Airflow, Bazel, Github CI/CD, and Spark, prior experience with these specific solutions is not needed. source_jobs.title: Director, Sales & Partnerships
source_jobs.description:

Since 2019, we have built one of the largest consented behavioral datasets in the United States, extending far beyond the walled gardens of Big Tech. Engineers here own major surface areas, build 0→1 systems in large-scale data and model infrastructure, and help define how Behavioral AI scales ethically and effectively.

 - Well-funded with 5+ years of runway. At the same time, we are scaling revenue quickly and project to be breakeven in 2026.

 - Partnerships with Microsoft and Databricks

 - Fully remote or hybrid from several hubs (SF Bay Area, Seattle, NYC)

 - World-class team of Machine Learning experts who worked on cutting edge infra and recommender systems @ Amazon, Uber, Twitter, Meta, etc.

 - Product and Go-To-Market teams who have taken ideas from concept to 9 figure revenue streams
   
   

Benefits:

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Meaningful equity & financial upside - a real % of the company

 - Annual bonus target based on personal and company performance

 - Health, Dental, Vision available

 - Unlimited PTO - we care about impact, not tracking days you’re out

 - 401k with company match %

Director, Sales & Partnerships
We're seeking a Director, Sales & Partnerships to drive Yobi's revenue with advertisers and brands by going to market through our growing roster of strategic alliances — including Microsoft, Databricks, major agency holding companies, large independent agencies, and others. This is a highly senior IC role for an experienced enterprise seller who can both close activation revenue with brands and deepen long-term, co-sell motions with our partners. The ideal candidate brings hands-on enterprise AdTech and/or MarTech sales experience, a strong track record of partner-led selling, and the drive to make a direct impact in a high-growth startup environment.
Responsibilities

 - Drive new revenue with advertisers and brands by co-selling through Yobi's strategic alliances, including Microsoft, Databricks, agency holding companies, large independent agencies, and others

 - Build and execute joint go-to-market plans in partnership with Yobi's Partnerships team and our alliance partners — including pipeline development, co-marketing motions, and aligned account planning into shared brand targets

 - Own enterprise activation sales cycles end-to-end with brand advertisers, from prospecting through close, leveraging partner relationships to accelerate deals

 - Expand existing alliances by unlocking new business units, agencies within HoldCo networks, and partner sales teams Yobi hasn't yet activated

 - Partner with junior account executives to coach, develop, and co-sell on alliance-sourced opportunities

 - Collaborate closely with Business Development and Product to align go-to-market strategy with partner roadmaps and brand client needs

 - Serve as a credible voice for Yobi inside partner organizations — getting our name into seller plays, enablement decks, and joint pitches

This role reports directly to the EVP, Commercial as Yobi AI builds its founding sales team to scale adoption of Yobi's business.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Client Partner
source_jobs.description:
Yobi is a rapidly growing Behavioral AI company on a mission to ethically democratize the benefits of data and AI.



Since 2019, we have built one of the largest consented behavioral datasets in the United States, extending far beyond the walled gardens of Big Tech. Unlike traditional LLM companies, Yobi builds foundation models of human behavior grounded in real-world actions such as purchases and store visits.



Our private-by-design modeling enables state-of-the-art personalization and decisioning for leading brands and agencies while protecting privacy, safety, and ethics.



Today, we are focused on bringing the performance of closed-web user acquisition to the open web and connected TV, giving brands walled-garden results without the walls.



At our core, Yobi is building the behavioral intelligence layer for any system that makes a personalization decision.



Working at Yobi

We’re at an inflection point—customer adoption is accelerating, but there’s still room to shape the architecture and culture from the ground up. Engineers here own major surface areas, build 0→1 systems in large-scale data and model infrastructure, and help define how Behavioral AI scales ethically and effectively.



Highlights:

 - Well-funded with 5+ years of runway. At the same time, we are scaling revenue quickly and project to be breakeven in 2026.

 - Partnerships with Microsoft and Databricks

 - Fully remote or hybrid from several hubs (SF Bay Area, Seattle, NYC)

 - World-class team of Machine Learning experts who worked on cutting edge infra and recommender systems @ Amazon, Uber, Twitter, Meta, etc.

 - Product and Go-To-Market teams who have taken ideas from concept to 9 figure revenue streams
   
   

Benefits:

 - Competitive Base Salary

 - Meaningful equity & financial upside - a real % of the company

 - Annual bonus target based on personal and company performance

 - Health, Dental, Vision available

 - Unlimited PTO - we care about impact, not tracking days you’re out

 - 401k with company match %

The Role
Yobi is hiring an Enterprise Client Partner to drive growth for enterprise clients by aligning their marketing goals with Yobi’s offering. This is a highly strategic, senior IC role tasked to:

 - Partner with Yobi’s sales team to be a trusted partner throughout the sales process, POC, and post-close.

 - Own long-term client outcomes for client success and revenue growth.

 - Collaborate with senior client leadership and teams (Brand, Performance, Retention, CRM, Data, Analytics, etc.) to unify efforts around growth.

 - Translate business objectives into long-term marketing roadmaps powered by Yobi’s algorithms and data.

 - Develop action plans that ensure measurable impact and internal execution.

 - Own client relationships and own the long-term adoption of Yobi’s product.

What We’re Looking For

 - A breadth of understanding of AdTech and MarTech solutions: bidding, creative, measurement, audiences, inventory, etc.

 - Extensive experience working with enterprise client teams, inside and outside of marketing.

 - Deep understanding of enterprise marketing across brand, performance, and retention.

 - Someone who thrives in new environments, is actively looking to build products & functions from 0 to 1, and is hungry to make an impact in a startup environment.

 - Experience working with customer datasets (CRM, 3P audiences, log-level data, etc.).

 - Balance of creative vision and hands-on execution.

 - Business acumen to be able to translate marketing KPIs to business outcomes.

 - Patience and willingness to see a sales cycle last 6-12+ months

 - NYC location preferred, but not required.
